recent_calls.summary: Morgan called after hours to check if a technician was still scheduled to come to their house tonight. The agent, Evie, explained that they could not check the live schedule or connect to a representative but documented the request for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended without further assistance as the user hung up.

The user called to cancel an appointment for today. The agent informed the user that cancellations cannot be processed immediately after hours but assured that the request would be forwarded to the team for follow-up on the next business day. The user confirmed the cancellation request and ended the call.

Charlotte called to report an active water leak from her HVAC air handler and expressed frustration that a technician had not arrived despite being told they would come three hours ago. The agent documented the emergency and informed Charlotte that no after-hours dispatch or live transfer was possible, but the team would follow up the next business day. Charlotte was upset about the delay and lack of immediate assistance.

Clark from American Home Shield called to inquire about an AC work order and specifically asked about a dispatch number and additional charges. The agent, Sophie, provided assistance but did not have billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for further help.

The user inquired about the technician's arrival time and asked the agent to relay a message to Amy. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user called inquiring about a scheduling call they received. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to request air conditioning maintenance service and provided their full address and role as a renter. The agent attempted to gather contact information but the user requested to speak to a live representative, leading to a successful call transfer.

The user called to report that their air conditioning is not turning on and provided their service address ZIP code. The agent confirmed the location and asked about the type of service needed, but the call ended abruptly after the user repeated themselves without further response.
The user called in and mentioned receiving a call, but the conversation was brief and the agent transferred the call to another number for further assistance.
The user called to follow up on some estimates and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 17 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.

The user, Anne, called to change her appointment time for a plumbing service to fix a valve. The agent successfully booked her for Tuesday, May 19th at 8 AM and informed her about the standard dispatch fee. Anne declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

The user, Mikaela Howard, reported an air conditioning issue that is not cooling and requested service as soon as possible through her home warranty. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address in Florissant, Missouri, and informed Mikaela that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and ended the call politely.

The user, Anne Schulz, called to schedule a repair appointment for a broken valve on her Rinnai tankless water heater. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for Monday, May 18th at 4 PM. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback.
The user returned a call from Budget and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted briefly and ended with a successful transfer.
The user, Carolyn, called to follow up on a recent air conditioner service. The agent identified the purpose of the call and transferred Carolyn to a live agent who could assist further.

The user called to confirm their appointment, and the agent verified that the appointment is scheduled for Monday, May 18th, between 10 AM and 12 PM. The user did not request any changes or additional assistance and expressed satisfaction with the information provided.

The user called to request emergency AC service as their unit was running but not cooling. The agent scheduled a service appointment for Monday at 10 AM and confirmed the user's contact details and service address. The user provided positive feedback about the agent's helpfulness and clarity.

The user called to inquire about the arrival time of a service technician, referencing a previous call where they were told someone would be sent out immediately. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user called to return a phone call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

The user informed the agent about a serious leak requiring immediate attention and provided an entry code for the repairman. The agent acknowledged the urgency and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The user expressed urgency and frustration about waiting for help.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another representative. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
The user called back and was greeted by the agent, who then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was completed successfully.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Jen from American Home Shield called Sophie at Budget to discuss an expedited work order and requested assistance with a technician service repair. Sophie acknowledged the work order number and transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user called to schedule a plumbing appointment for a leak near their hot water heater. The agent successfully booked the appointment for Monday, May 18th at 2 PM and collected all necessary details. The user was satisfied with the service and provided positive feedback.

The user called to verify if an estimate for upgrading their A/C unit was still valid and inquired about financing options. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about a previous quote for an air conditioning unit provided through their insurance. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user requested to speak with a real person, and the agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.
The user requested to speak with a real person, and the agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without further interaction.
The user requested to speak with a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak with a real person, and the agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.
The user requested to speak with a real person instead of an AI, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Morgan Poppelwell, called to confirm an emergency AC repair request due to a health concern with his pregnant wife and high indoor temperature. The agent verified the details, including the home warranty dispatch number, and assured that someone would contact him shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the call ended positively.

Julie Sutter called to report a leak coming from underneath her air conditioning unit in her mobile home, causing water to spread across the hallway floor. The agent collected her details and attempted to transfer her to a live emergency specialist, but the transfer failed. The agent assured Julie that her issue was escalated and that an emergency specialist would call her back promptly.

The user expressed frustration with the AI agent not responding and requested to speak with a real person for immediate assistance. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent. The call ended with a successful transfer to a human representative.

Charlotte called to report a leaking air handler in her ceiling and requested urgent HVAC service to stop the leak before it damages her floor. The agent informed her that the earliest available appointments are on Monday, but Charlotte insisted on service today. The call ended abruptly after Charlotte expressed frustration about the lack of same-day availability.

The user called to inquire about weekend service assignments and to report an issue with their AC unit not cooling properly. The agent collected detailed information including the user's name, address, email, and home warranty dispatch number, and confirmed the service request. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided neutral feedback, indicating a preference for speaking with a human.

Levi from American Home called to request expedited emergency HVAC service due to a leaking air conditioner and non-functioning motor, which is critical because of a health issue related to the customer's wife's pregnancy. The agent documented the emergency details, escalated the request, and informed Levi that a technician would be contacted on the next business day since it was before 8 AM. The call ended with confirmation of contact details and no further assistance needed.

Michayla called to report a large leak above the hot water heater inside her store and requested emergency plumbing service. The agent documented the issue and escalated it as urgent but could not dispatch a plumber after hours. Michayla then canceled the request, and the agent confirmed the cancellation and closed the call politely.

The user, Jin Suh, called to schedule an emergency service for their AC unit which is running but not cooling, causing discomfort. The agent collected all necessary details and informed Jin that the service team will contact them on the next business day to confirm the appointment, prioritizing the request due to the discomfort. The call ended with no further questions or instructions from the user.

The user inquired about whether their membership covers a summer or spring cooling checkup. The agent informed the user that coverage depends on the specific plan and noted the request for a follow-up contact to clarify the membership details. The user declined to schedule an appointment and requested a callback for more information.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user but no further conversation occurred before the user hung up.

The user, Dan, called to request a status update on a heating and cooling service request. The agent, Evie, documented the request and informed Dan that a team member would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from Dan.

Gregory called to schedule a regular service appointment for his cooling system, which is not cooling properly. The agent noted his preference for an early Monday appointment and confirmed the service address. The team will follow up next business day to confirm the appointment time.

The user called to make an emergency appointment for their AC unit under a home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details including the dispatch number and confirmed the appointment for the next day. The user expressed interest in home maintenance program information and the call ended positively with no issues.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user, Betty, initiated a call and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The agent attempted to connect Betty with a representative to address her needs.

The user called to inquire about having someone check their air conditioner and ductwork and asked about potential charges related to text messages from Budget Heating and Cooling. The agent explained that data rates might apply for text messages and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.
